comments.title: Heathen Rosary Outline and Planetary Strength in Astrological Charts
comments.content:
Good Evening!

I hope everyone had an enjoyable and/or restful weekend and has a great week to look forward to.

To Share: I am still working on my "Heathen Rosary". I don't have all of the prayers written, but I have the overall framework worked out. I share what that looks like and some of the thoughts behind it here, in case that's helpful to anyone: https://jpowellrussell.com/#framework_for_a_heathen_rosary.

To Ask: If I might, three hopefully quick questions on planetary strength/dignity in astrological charts:

1. I know that when two planets are in mutual reception, each is treated as having the dignity of his or her rulership. Should each planet be interpreted as if he were actually in the sign of his rulership? An example, in case I've been unclear: If Jupiter and Uranus are in mutual reception, should I interpret Jupiter as very strong, but in Aquarius, or as if he were in Sagittarius (and vice versa, of course)?

2. Does being the ruler of a chart give a planet any dignity, or does it just say that planet needs to be paid attention to?

3. Are there any rough guidelines to the magnitude of different strengths/dignities and weaknesses/debilities? I know that everything in astrology has to be interpreted in context, but I'm having a hard time working out mixed dignities. An example from a chart I'm trying to get a handle on: Mars is in Aries and so in his rulership (dignity/strength), he's conjunct Venus (dignity/strength, since she's a benefic), but is square Neptune (a debility), and in the Ninth House (Cadent, so a debility). My initial impression is that this is "pretty strong, but with some complications", but I'm not sure if it is instead more of a wash (balanced dignity and debility).

As always, thanks very much for providing this invaluable resource,
Jeff

replies.title: Re: Heathen Rosary Outline and Planetary Strength in Astrological Charts
replies.content:
1) I treat them as having the strength they would have in the sign of their rulership.

2) I don't give a planet dignity for this.

3) Nope. That's something that has to be learnt by the systematic study of charts.

replies.title: Re: Heathen Rosary Outline and Planetary Strength in Astrological Charts
replies.content:
1) and 2) - Thanks much! That's about what I figured.

3) Aw shucks, unfortunately, that is also about what I figured, but I was hoping for an easier headstart.

Much appreciated,
Jeff

replies.title: Re: Heathen Rosary Outline and Planetary Strength in Astrological Charts
replies.content: 3) Looking over this question in the gray light of morning, I should have been more explicit. (My head was full of details from a scene in the novel I'm writing, if that's any excuse.) The reason you can't just tot up the dignities and debilities via some kind of scheme is that they differ in quality, not just in quantity. A square with Neptune involves completely different downsides from a square with Mars or the Moon, and it also varies depending on the sign and house in which the planets involved are placed! It can be useful, if you don't mind putting a few years into study and meditation, to meditate on what each planet means in each sign, and what each positive and negative aspect between planets means in general. It differs in every case.

replies.title: Re: Heathen Rosary Outline and Planetary Strength in Astrological Charts
replies.content:
3) Ah, thanks very much for this, that helps clarify quite a bit. So far I've mostly been approaching astrology pretty casually - reading your mundane posts, trying to make sense of the natal charts of folks I know well, that kind of thing. Sounds like it might be a good option to explore during my break from advanced magical practice for the next few years, and this gives me some idea how to put together a course of study and meditation.

Cheers,
Jeff

replies.title: Re: Orphic Hymn Karaokes
replies.content:
Not a dumb question, but - Athena is a Greek goddess of wisdom and also of war hen necessary. The Greek myth has her born from the head of Zeus with no human mother; other variations give her a mother whom Zeus swallowed lest she bear him a son who would supplant him. (Modern psychologists and novelists dealing with the dynamics of corporate families have mined some nuggets from that latter.) She was also the layer who defended Orestes against the charge of matricide.

Minerva is Roman, and probably Etruscan in origin. There was a temple to "The Capitoline Trio: Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva," which apparently defines her place in the divine royal family. Another threefold temple, I've forgotten the name of it, was reared to Jupiter, Mars, and Quirinus; I read this as Jupiter at Work vs Jupiter at home.

The Romans tended to equate other peoples' gods with their own, and added the Greek myths to their Roman counterparts; later writes like Bulfinch gave the Roman- equivalent names of the Greek gods in his Mythology, even when the myths were definitely Greek; "Minerva's temple in Athens...." frex.

Does that help?

comments.title: A contemplative method for grounding
comments.content:
Greetings JMG and commentariat!

Can I share an experiment I did?

The past couple of months were stressful in the extreme for me and it seems stress can make the psychic senses kick in an unbalanced manner. I also noted that for a few days my practices left me warm, like when you have a few sips of something hot, but a little uncomfortable instead. All that is Fire. So I came up with something that used Earth and Water to put it off, tested it and it worked really well for me! I'm sharing it here in case somebody finds it useful. If it works or it doesn't work or it gave you ideas or questions I'll be grateful to know.

For a quicker version of this, though specific for the heating part, drink cool, not cold, ash guard juice aka "winter melon". You can also apply castor oil in the points mentioned and go your way, or both. There are many things many people suggest to apply to these point with different effects, the ones I give below are meant to cool you down. Whatever you do, don't use any sort of ash as it would likely make it worse.

Materials:

1) A Copper ring
2) Soil

Steps:

1. Clean the copper ring with lime juice and salt. Metals shouldn't be worn when they have an oxide layer and particularly copper. Don't use soap or any synthetic cleaners. You can put the ring in a small container and fill it with lemon or lime juice, it will be mostly clean after 30 minutes. Then scrub it with salt to get the rest off or you could add lime and salt to a paper cloth and clean it with that if you don't want to wait. Tamarind paste also works. The lemon juice has probably changed color into a shade of green if the ring was dirty. Throw this away.

2. Ask an Earth or fertility goddess to bless the ring for grounding and removing bad energies whatever way you like or if you prefer, just ask the Earth itself. Venus is a good option but there are many. The ring should be worn on the left ring finger of the left hand. If it happens to be a copper snake ring, as they are popular, the head of the snake should point to the finger tips, not towards you. You can wear the ring for this only, or often if you clean it daily, don't sleep with it as it might leave a green stain if your skin PH is alkaline. If you forget, don't worry, just clean the stain off your finger the same way you did the ring.

3. Get a handful of soil from a pot or your garden, or other place where people don't walk on a regular basis, we want the energies of the earth undisturbed. Compost-soil works well just be sure it's fully composted and doesn't smell but of fresh soil.

4. Add clean water to the soil for it to be moist enough to hold a shape but not turn liquid.

5. Say a short prayer or just try to settle putting everything down mentally, emotionally and in the tension of your body. So basically relax or try to relax. Many times just trying to relax is more than enough in order to relax.

6. When you are ready, put a little bit of the mud in a plate or your left palm and add more water until it turns liquid. With the ring finger of the right hand put a generous amount of the soil mixture on the following points doing a slight massage in circles. Try both directions and also shifting your focus between your skin and your fingers. See what feels better, note if any of those feels unwinding. If it hurts, press a little harder and massage for a little more. The points are: an inch below your navel; where the rib cage meets; the pit of the throat; the forehead; and the soft spot behind the ears. To find the soft spot behind the ears place a finger on the back of your neck where it meets the skull, at the height of the ears. Move to either side towards the ear and when you feel the bone, just backtrack a little and put some mud paste on that soft spot on both sides.

7. Now, with your hands make a ball of mud and hold it with both hands for a bit in front of you comfortably. Focus on The Earth and follow your thoughts. For example: feel her generosity, your whole material body and the entire planet is made out of Earth in its milliards of combinations. She takes in filth and decay and produces life and harmony with it. If you feel dirty, worked up or unbalanced, you just have to reestablish this connection. This is a gift we are all given by the Earth.

8. Hold the mud ball tight, but not stiff on your hand and go and take an unplanned walk like that, with a mid-slow but steady pace, looking slightly downwards. Try both hands for a few minutes and see what feels more relaxing for you. Yes, there is mud in your face. If it makes you uncomfortable that people might see you like that dust it off a little bit or wear a cap. We don't want to add any more stress, but if you can, leave as much of the mud as possible or just put a dot of mud paste in between your eye brows instead. Alternatively, you could pace up and down a hallway or other walking path, preferably close to a natural space.

9. Give thanks and wash away the mud ball on a river or on running water, burry it in your front yard or next to a big tree.

10. Go and do something else that you enjoy.

It's elaborate, but it works. I should probably note, since it's been mentioned a few times, that if the sensation of heat doesn't go away after a while you should stop magical practice and leave your nervous system rest.

Oh and LA is impressively psychically and energetically filthy. Banishing twice daily is worth considering.

In your Ars Memorativa article, you show that you need a fixed location/landscape, a set of images for the letters of the alphabet, and a set of images of numbers. Obviously the Ogham themselves can handle all three of those things.

I'm not sure however to go about it. What I thought so far I could do was to imagine a place with a few different types of landscape with a winding path through them, so an open field at the bottom of a hill, the hill itself, a cliff at one side of it, a pond, an old forest, etc, perhaps one landscape per aicme. I could then put the Ogham trees in this landscape, in order. I could then associate images with letters and numbers and put them into the trees.

Ideally I would use the trees of each few themselves to stand for English letters, but not all the English letters have fews, and indeed there are 25 fews versus 26 English letters. Numbers are easier, I could perhaps use standing stones with a few for each of the first 9 numbers, and then perhaps use Koad for zero. There are the correspondences in the Druidry Handbook to use, too, but these are also named after only a subset of English letters.

Any advice would be most welcome, I am probably overcomplicating things! Thanks in advance.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
The ars memorativa paper was based on one particular method of mnemonics, the one that uses visualized landscapes. The old Ogham mnemonic system uses a different method.

1) Memorize the sequence of Ogham letters, so you don't even have to think about their order -- it comes automatically.

2) Memorize several lists of things that begin with the relevant letters in order. That's why the Auraicept na n-Eces has the lists of trees, herbs, birds, etc. with the first letters of each Ogham letter. If you're fluent in Irish you can certainly use those; if not, as the same text says, invent your own: Bucket, Ladle, and so on. Commit those to memory.

3) To memorize a sequence of things, associate them with the images from one of the lists. If you have a shopping list to remember, imagine item #1 in a bucket, item #2 with a ladle sitting on top of it, and so on.

4) Keep adding lists of objects as needed, committing them to memory and using them to mentally mark sequences of things you want to remember.

Interesting, that also implies I could use the Ogham type itself as another mnemonic device!

Is there a way you've found useful to get around the differences in English language letters? I double-checked, and one possibility is if I use K for Koad, and J for Oir, all the other letters in English are covered except W. So for W I could use two Ur images ("double U").

replies.content: Depends on your definition of "occult group." The Shingon sect of Japanese Buddhism, which is chockfull of Buddhist occultism, has been continuously active since the year 809; there are traditions in India and China that are older than that. In the West, the violent persecution of occultism made habits of secrecy and dispersal mandatory, and those didn't even begin to wear off until the 18th century; the oldest Western occult group I know of that stil exists today is the Order de Elus Coens, which was founded in 1761. (One Druid group claims to date back to 1717 but there's no documentation for that.)

Hello,

He seems to have been an honest and remarkable man, but I often wonder how accurate his observations of his own experience were. He strikes me as a very interesting character because he seems to have come across his philosophy on his own without too much connection to the western occult tradition, yet he wound up influencing the tradition a great deal.

I don't know how familiar you are with Swedenborg's work, but, given his importance to Western Occultism, I was wondering if you could share your thoughts are on where you think Swedenborg was accurate and where he erred in his study of the unseen. replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Swedenborg and Steiner are the two great eccentric visionaries of modern Western occultism. I've studied both, and I recommend this, because they contradict each other quite often. That makes it hard to get caught in the trap of mistaking visionary experience for objective fact.

Dion Fortune's comment regarding The Cosmic Doctrine applies with equal force to the visionary cosmology of Swedenborg: "these images are meant to train the mind, not to inform it." Approach Swedenborg's teachings as a set of metaphors meant to communicate realities that cannot be communicated in any other way, and they're helpful; treat them as literally true and you've just landed in Dogmaville, and that's not a useful place to be.

comments.title: Oddly timed Earthquakes
comments.content:
Less than an hour ago, a strong Earthquake (preliminary reports put its magnitude in the 6.8-7.5 range, Richter scale) strikes Central Mexico on 09/19. Again.

The original 09/19 quake happened in 1985, and caused huge losses, but human and material. Since then, the culture of emergency response has been transformed in Mexico, with large scale evacuation drills taking place on the aniversary in all schools, public buildings and many corporate buildings. Also since then, small September quakes are relatively common (I do not have good memories prior to the late 80's to compare if this was the case before or not).

Occasionally, stronger earthquakes do happen on the precise date 09/19. The most relevant one was in 2017, and this is posed to go to the records too. It is too early to say how bad it hit, but to me it felt, if anything, stronger than 2017's.

That we are going to have earthquakes is a given: we are practically siting on top of the volcanic fire belt of the Americas. That they happen at the end of the rain season may have also physical explanations. I do not know a thing about vulcanology, but it sounds plausible: same conditions -> same outcomes.

But, what is it with 09/19? Are all the drills and simulacrums working like a ritual to summon quakes? Is there an astrological/numerological explanation instead?

comments.content:
This may be a better question for a philosopher than for a Druid, but I don’t know of anyone who has Philosophy Phriday, so here I am on Magic Monday.

We have all had those road-not-taken moments: this job or that job? This house or that house? Have the surgery or not? We make what we hope is the best decision we can. But in these road-not-taken decisions, part of your mind wants to go one way, part the other. Let’s say I’m trying to decide between 2 white houses, each of which has ONE desirable feature the other lacks. One has southwest-facing windows, we’ll say, and the other has first-floor laundry hookups, as opposed to the more common basement hookups. So I ponder and ponder and finally decide.

What I’ve always wondered is: in these situations, is there a “me” in there somewhere who got overridden? Is there another “me” who kept trying to yell “But what about the green house on the snowplow route?” and never even got a hearing? How many of “me” are there, and where does a “me” go once “I” decide something else?

More practically, let’s say that after many snowy winters I decide green-house “me” was right and when a decision needs to be made, she should be the one who makes it. Could I somehow become green-house “me,” and, if so, what happens to any others?

—Princess Cutekitten

comments.title: the knights of labor
comments.content:
Archdruid,

Was Uriah Smith Stephen, the founder of the knights of labor, an occultist?

replies.title: Re: the knights of labor
replies.content: Not as far as I've ever heard. Remember that fraternal lodges were a very common form of social organization in those days, and many of them had rituals that would seem very occult to a person today but simply looked like ordinary symbolic ceremony back then -- the 19th century hadn't yet forgotten how to think in symbolic terms. So Stephens and his Knights of Labor were simply doing what other social organizations did at that time.

comments.title: Creating places on the astral plane
comments.content: I wish to learn about creating places and objects on the astral level. Is there a go-to source for this that you consider particularly good?

replies.title: Re: Creating places on the astral plane
replies.content: No, I don't know of one, and it's unlikely that one will be written, since the art of astral creation is a very personal thing. If you do a lot of pathworking and scrying, and train your ability to visualize by regular practice, you'll learn what you need to know.
